---
title: "Movement Disorder Emergencies"
---

MOVEMENT DISORDER EMERGENCIES
ACUTE PSYCHOSIS IN PARKINSON’S DISEASE
Diagnosis
1. Exclude secondary causes including infection (UTI, PNA,etc), metabolic derangements, medications.
Treatment
1. Treat secondary cause if one exists.
2. Gradually reduce exacerbating PD medications (amantadine > dopamine agonists > COMT/MAOI > levodopa)
3. Cautious symptomatic treatment of psychosis with the least D2 blocking agents [quetiapine > clozapine].
PARKINSONISM-HYPERPYREXIA SYNDROME
Signs and symptoms
1. Indistinguishable from Neuroleptic Malignant Syndrome and often due to abrupt w/d of dopaminergic agents.
Diagnosis
1. Exclude other etiologies: infection, metabolic derangements, medications (esp. dopamine blockers).
Treatment
1. Restart dopaminergic agents.
2. If no PO access, establish PO access with temporary gastric tube (Nasogastric, Duotube).
a. OK to crush Sinemet IR. May need to temporarily replace Sinemet CR with IR.
(dose of IR is approx. ½ of CR dose)
3. If unable to use gut, may temporarily use apomorphine SC if available and only if necessary.
a. Starting dose 2mg SC, max 5 doses per day. pretreatment with trimethobenzamide to avoid nausea.
INPATIENT OR PERIOPERATIVE TREATMENT OF PARKINSONS DISEASE
Treatment
1. Restart dopaminergic agents.
2. If no PO access, establish PO access with temporary gastric tube (Nasogastric, Duotube).
a. OK to crush Sinemet IR. May need to temporarily replace Sinemet CR with IR.
(dose of IR is approx. ½ of CR dose)
3. If unable to use gut, may temporarily use apomorphine SC if available and only if necessary.
a. Starting dose 2mg SC, max 5 doses per day. pretreatment with trimethobenzamide to avoid nausea.
GENERAL RECOMMENDATIONS FOR TREATMENT OF MOVEMENTS DISORDERS
Robottom et al. Arch Neurol. June 2011.


![](images/movement_disorder_emergencies_page59.png)
